title: #67 - My phone is fixed - Leaving Zurich.

blocks.text: Hey I didnt forget about this dont worry. MORE chronicals are coming.

blocks.caption: 25Km, Zurich to some bush in the forest...

blocks.text: We left off the last post on August 1st, national swiss day, with a long 170km bike day in the rain, a broken iphone, and the 1 in a 100,000 chance of bumping into Adam in Zurich just after I had left him in the Alps a few days before due to his injury.
I woke up in house of my dear cycling/doctor friends, Lukas and his girlfriend. I enjoyed an amazing sleep in in a nice bed. A comfortable change from the nights in the mountains.

I remember Lukas's girlfriend making me a coffee before I left for the day.Lukas the night before had helped me write down detailed instructions for how to get to an iPhone repair store without google maps. I had written them all down in my journal and memorized them. "Go to the lake, 5 bridged up from the lake, to the left there are 6 iphone repair shops"It was easy to find! Who needs GPS navigation anyway?
The service was great, it was an apple store. They said they cold change the phone with only a slight risk that the phone would be completley wiped.

In those four hours I biked around Zurich without my phone, I found a quiet place at the university of Zurich where I took a lovely nap on some grass. When I woke up from the nap I realized I lost one of my running shoes somewhere in town... It was extra disapointing because i had just bought them new for this trip. Oh well, no more shoes for me.
I only had one shoe left which I decided I would carry with me until I fully left Zurich just incase another miracle happened and I found the other.
I went back to the repair shop to find that they changed the screen out and gave it back to me fully repaired. Good as new... and I still had all my trip photos.
Once it was fixed I slowly started biking out of Zurich to Basel.

Leaving Zurich I biked along the river, the people who live in Zurich frequently swim in the river and float down it, jumping off the bridges. It looked like a very peaceful way to spend a summer day. I would have tried it but I felt a rush to get moving and leave the city.I continued along the gravel of the river and felt very very tired and low energy. I thought I could make it at least half way to Basel (about 90 km to basel) but about 15 km into biking beside the river, I got tired and found a place to camp, cooked, watched an italian TV show on Netflix and slept.
In Switzerland wild camping is illegal... So I hid in a bush off the cycling path where no one could see me.

blocks.caption: My smiling after my phone was fixed.
Hiding in a bush to sleep
